meta_title: For Students: client workflow to Solve can't debug code (Beginner Resource Page)
meta_description: Learn how to client workflow and overcome can't debug code. A Beginner-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Mastering Client Workflows to Debug AI Code
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with bugs. Learn a professional client workflow using Antigravity and Claude Code to build your first AI apps with confidence and speed.
content.answer_block: Debugging isn't about trial and error; it's about systematic isolation. By implementing a structured client workflow, you force your code to reveal its state at every transition. Use Antigravity to map logic flows and Claude Code to automate the trace, turning mysterious crashes into actionable, solved tickets.
content.intro:
You are staring at a screen of red text, the cursor blinking like a heartbeat you can't restore. You've spent hours chasing a ghost in your codebase, feeling the weight of the 'beginner' label pressing down on your ambition. Every developer has been in this dark room, wondering if they are cut out for the complexity of real-world software.

But here is the secret: the difference between a stuck student and a lead engineer isn't raw intelligence—it's the workflow. When you treat debugging as a structured engineering process rather than a guessing game, the code stops being an adversary and starts being a blueprint. Let’s transform your panic into a repeatable, professional system.

steps.title: Define the Execution Boundary
steps.description: Before fixing, you must isolate the failing module to prevent scope creep. This ensures you are debugging a single, predictable function.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze this file and create a dependency graph of the current function. Identify all external state mutations.

steps.title: Instrument the Trace
steps.description: Inject logging at the entry and exit points of your logic to observe variable state changes in real-time.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Insert structured log statements at the start and end of every function in this block, capturing input arguments and return values.

steps.title: Execute Atomic Tests
steps.description: Run the specific function in isolation to verify the output matches your expected schema.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a unit test for this function that covers the edge case that currently causes the crash.

steps.title: Automate Root Cause Analysis
steps.description: Feed the stack trace and the test output into the AI to identify the logical divergence.
steps.antigravity_prompt: I have the following stack trace and logs. Identify the exact line where the state deviates from the requirements.

steps.title: Validate and Commit
steps.description: Once the fix is applied, verify it against the full suite and commit the progress to your portfolio.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review the patch for this bug and suggest any refactoring to prevent this class of error in the future.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --commit-fix --message='Resolved state mutation bug in core logic'

pro_tips: Treat every bug as a feature request for better logging; if you can't see it, it doesn't exist.
Never fix a bug without writing a regression test; it proves to employers you care about software stability.
Use 'Rubber Ducking' with Claude Code by explaining the code out loud before asking for a fix; it forces your brain to organize the logic.
Keep your functions under 20 lines; if it's longer, it's too complex to debug effectively.
Standardize your error handling across the app to make stack traces predictable and readable.
resources: Clean Code Principles for Beginners: https://refactoring.guru
Debugging Strategies for SaaS Apps: https://sentry.io/resources
content.next_steps: After mastering this workflow, apply it to a new project feature. Push your code to GitHub, document the bug fix in your README, and share your progress with the community to start building your professional reputation.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical blueprint to stop guessing and start building. This workflow is the difference between a student who gets stuck and a developer who ships. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
